content.full_text:
TO: 
FROM: 
DATE: 
SUBJECT: 
Barbara Milman 
Chief Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Vance Raye 
Governor's Office 
May 1, 1985 
Political Reform Act Reporting Obligations 
This will confirm our telephone conversation of May 1, 1985 
regarding the Governor's reporting obligation with respect to 
donated aircraft services which were utilized for a personal trip. 
Although we did not discuss the nature of the trip, you may be 
interested in knowing that the trip was taken to attend a 
relative's funeral. 
You advised that the usual rules pertaining to reimbursement 
for air transportation apply to personal as well as State business 
or campaign trips: 
If the Governor reimburses the provider of the 
transportation at the scheduled commercial air rate, there is no 
obligation to report the value of the trip on the Governor's 
Statement of Economic Interests. 
Thank you for your advice on this matter.

sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This appears to be an outgoing correspondence/thank you letter from the Governor's Office to FPPC, not a formal FPPC advice letter. It references and confirms a prior telephone conversation about reporting obligations but does not contain formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The document summarizes advice given verbally rather than providing new written analysis.
